entities.name: SOYBEAN
entities.description: Glycine max (L.) Merr., the focus crop of the study known for symbiotic nitrogen fixation.

entities.name: DROUGHT
entities.description: An environmental factor characterized by water deficit that contributes to crop yield loss.

entities.name: CROP YIELD
entities.description: The measure of agricultural productivity of the soybean crop.

entities.name: SYMBIOTIC N2 FIXATION
entities.description: The biological process of atmospheric nitrogen fixation in soybean plants.

entities.name: SOIL WATER DEFICIT
entities.description: A state of inadequate soil moisture that affects nitrogen fixation.

entities.name: PROTEIN PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of creating seed proteins, which is dependent on nitrogen availability.

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL SCREENING
entities.description: A preliminary screening process used to identify lines with N2 fixation drought tolerance.

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The research effort to develop soybean lines with diminished sensitivity of N2 fixation to drought.

entities.name: N2 FIXATION DROUGHT TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a soybean line to maintain nitrogen fixation activity under drought conditions.

entities.name: JACKSON
entities.description: A soybean cultivar proven to have N2 fixation tolerance to drought.

entities.name: KS4895
entities.description: A high-yielding soybean line used in crossing.

entities.name: YIELD TRIALS
entities.description: Tests conducted in moderate- and low-yielding rainfed environments to evaluate soybean lines.

entities.name: RAINFED ENVIRONMENTS
entities.description: Agricultural environments where water is supplied solely by precipitation.

entities.name: GREENHOUSE
entities.description: A controlled environment used to test N2 fixation activity in drying soil.
